FSM_STATUS

Table for storing FSM status definitions per class type.

Columns
fst_id (according to `pit_util.ora_name_type`)

Primary key.

fst_fcl_id (according to `pit_util.ora_name_type`)

Primary key, reference to FSM_CLASSES.

fst_fsg_id (according to `pit_util.ora_name_type`)

Optional grouping criterion, reference to FSM_STATUS_GROUPS.

fst_msg_id (according to `pit_util.ora_name_type`)

Name of the message to be used for logging, reference to PIT_MESSAGE.

fst_pti_id (according to `pit_util.ora_name_type`)

Translatable item, reference to PIT_TRANSLATABLE_ITEM.

fst_retries_on_error (`number`)

Number of retries to perform in case of error. Default: 0.

fst_retry_schedule (according to `pit_util.ora_name_type`)

Optional schedule to use when retries are enabled.

fst_retry_time (`number`)

Optional waiting time before retrying.

fst_warn_interval (`interval day to second`)

Optional lower threshold for escalation to WARN.

fst_alert_interval (`interval day to second`)

Optional upper threshold for escalation to ALERT.

fst_escalation_basis (according to `pit_util.ora_name_type`)

Determines whether elapsed time is measured from the last status change or the last successful event.

fst_icon_css (according to `pit_util.ora_name_type`)

Optional icon to control the display in reports.

fst_name_css (according to `pit_util.ora_name_type`)

Optional CSS class to control the display in reports.

fst_initial_status (according to `pit_util.flag_type`)

Flag indicating whether this status is the initial status of a new FSM object.

fst_terminal_status (according to `pit_util.flag_type`)

Flag indicating whether this status is terminal.

fst_active (according to `pit_util.flag_type`)

Flag indicating whether the status is currently used.

Dependencies
fst_fcl_id

references FSM_CLASSES (fcl_id)

fst_fsg_id, fst_fcl_id

references FSM_STATUS_GROUPS (fsg_id, fsg_fcl_id)

fst_msg_id

references <PIT_MESSAGE> (pms_id)

fst_pti_id, fst_fcl_id

references <PIT_TRANSLATABLE_ITEM> (pti_uid, pti_upmg)
